entry.title: Washington Post: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Costly, scandal-ridden mass testing is China’s latest ‘zero covid’ bet
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukrainian forces pushed from Severodonetsk city’s center; NATO chief to Sweden
  • Afghans go hungry as U.S. and Taliban officials blame each other
  • North Korea fires artillery shells after Kim’s pledge to build up arms
  • Russia likely to seize control of eastern Ukrainian region within weeks, U.S. official says
  • Putin makes his imperial pretensions clear
  • De-arched former Moscow McDonald’s reopens without Big Macs
  • How Shireen Abu Akleh was killed
  • Hunting the hidden dangers that lurk in Ukraine’s lakes and rivers
  • European 'star survey' reveals celestial treasure trove
  • Pope bows out of annual procession due to knee pain
  • Bachelet won't seek 2nd term as UN human rights chief
  • Spain: 22 injured as engine collides with passenger train
  • Jihadi attacks mount in Burkina Faso despite junta's efforts
  • UN, elder: Week of tribal clashes in Sudan’s Darfur kill 100
  • Chinese Canadian pop star Kris Wu faces rape charge in court
  • 2 members of Iran's Revolutionary Guard die amid tensions
  • Iraq's 'pearl of the south' Lake Sawa dry amid water crisis
  • Belongings of missing men found tied underwater in Amazon
  • Most Americans oppose trans athletes in female sports, poll finds
  • A Minneapolis woman was about to be evicted. Neighbors bought her home for her.
  • Newspaper apologizes to Rebel Wilson over plans to out her relationship
  • Assistant police chief gets $1.5 million payout after hanging Nazi symbol
  • Men tied to hate group planned for riot, ‘confrontation’ at LGBTQ event, police say
  • Court filing offers new evidence of post-election breach in Coffee County, Ga.
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Committee to focus on how Trump’s ‘big lie’ fueled the insurrection
  • Inside Biden’s frustration with soaring prices
  • Next Jan. 6 hearings to focus on how Trump’s ‘big lie’ fueled rioters
  • Amazon calls cops, fires workers in attempts to stop unionization nationwide
  • You agreed to what? Doctor check-in software harvests your health data.
  • IRS rules about capital gains may lead to questions. We have answers.
  • Historic, four-bedroom house in Charles County lists at $355,000
  • The Oil Price Shock Will Reverberate Into Next Year
  • Facebook’s Greater Threat Is the Law, Not Lawsuits
  • The ECB Is No Longer an Inflation-Targeting Central Bank
  • Europe Is a Vast Idea. How Does Ukraine Fit in?
  • How the ‘Energy Shock’ Has Upended the Global Economy
  • How Biden Wants to Trim a Mountain of Student Debt
  • Didi’s Move From NYSE to Hong Kong — What to Know
  • Waive Pharma’s Vaccine Rights? What That Would Mean
  • We can’t let Ukraine lose. It needs a lot more aid, starting with artillery.
  • $5 gas is largely Putin’s fault
  • The San Francisco district attorney saga set back criminal justice reform
  • Personal finance class should be required in high school
  • 7 questions I want the Jan. 6 committee to answer at its upcoming hearings
  • Cartoon by Pia Guerra
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 13 Jun, 2022

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • April 1, 2003: Pop star Leslie Cheung dies in fall from Hong Kong hotel
  • 2 workers on gondola plunge about 20 floors to their deaths at Hong Kong residential block
  • August 22, 1999: China Airlines plane crashes at Hong Kong airport while landing amid typhoon
  • July 6, 1998: ‘Roger, over and out, Kai Tak’ - Hong Kong’s old airport closes
  • Is Hong Kong a great place to live? How quality of life changed post-handover
  • Departing Hong Kong property investors haunted by same dilemma of 1997, where will the best real estate returns be found?
  • Data on asylum seekers detained in Hong Kong should be disclosed more proactively, legal expert says
  • More than 60 per cent of coronavirus-related deaths in Hong Kong were ‘likely caused by Covid-19’, figure higher among older residents
  • Global capital flows and geopolitical risks are Hong Kong’s biggest challenges for the next few years, city’s de facto central banker says
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping calls on Hongkongers to better integrate with nation’s development
  • Hongkongers around the world urged on social media to gather to mark 3rd anniversary of 2019 social unrest
  • Nearly 1 in 4 young Hongkongers from low-income families ‘lying flat’, while some feel hopeless about future, survey shows
  • China’s largest academic paper database CNKI opens some services to individuals after Beijing’s antitrust probe
  • China and Australia break diplomatic drought with ‘frank’ defence ministers talks
  • Beijing’s ban on Taiwanese grouper imports prompts emergency measures to help island’s fish farmers
  • Chinese capital tightens Covid controls to stamp out spread of bar cluster
  • Huawei’s cloud service down for half an hour, disrupting services for Guangdong customers
  • What’s fuelling China’s lithium rush in Zimbabwe? The long game on zero carbon
  • Say it with aircraft carriers: why countries send in the big ships
  • Don’t turn South Pacific into US-China geopolitical ‘boxing ring’, Beijing says
  • China is wooing Pacific nations – but can it win trust?
  • Messy Summit of the Americas highlights contrast in US, China hosting styles
  • Peering between the pot plants at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
  • Sanctions are not the answer to Ukraine, North Korea, Chinese defence officials tell Shangri-La Dialogue
  • China ride-hailing giant Didi to start trading on OTC market after NYSE delisting, ending an 11-month fiasco that angered Beijing
  • Japan calls for ‘self-restraint’ from China after joint fly-past with Russian fighter jets
  • Taiwan’s health minister tests positive for Covid as new cases fall
  • Is Japan’s reopening to tourists triggering a series of ‘kamikaze car’ attacks between ‘yakuza’ gangs?
  • Japan passes bill to make online insults punishable by jail time, following suicide of Hana Kimura, star of Netflix show ‘Terrace House’
  • Singapore’s Lawrence Wong serves as acting PM as Lee Hsien Loong takes a week-long break
  • Ukraine war: India, China and other Asian nations growing markets for shunned Russian oil
  • Malaysia losing billions in sales as firms turn down orders due to migrant labour shortage
  • South Korea economy faces risks as trucker strike enters 7th day
  • Netflix confirms Squid Game will return for a second season
  • India police charge 30 soldiers for killing six tribal labourers
  • Indian officials step up arrests, demolish houses to stop unrest over anti-Islam remarks
  • Optimism in Indonesia as youth prospects improve amid economic recovery
  • Coronavirus: Travel firms in state of confusion as Japan reopens to foreign tourists
  • India’s Sonia Gandhi, opposition leader, hospitalised with Covid-related issues
  • North Korea fires suspected artillery pieces into sea, says Seoul military
  • Philippines: Tunnel drilling for first subway set to start, with Japanese machine and cash
  • Japan’s Fukushima village residents allowed to return 11 years after nuclear disaster - but do they want to?
  • UK to defy EU by scrapping Northern Ireland trade rules: ‘bureaucratic simplifications’, says Prime Minister Johnson
  • Russia’s ex-PM says Vladimir Putin ‘out of it’, Ukraine war could last two years
  • Google suspends engineer who publicly claimed he interacted with a sentient AI bot
  • One way out of Ukraine’s Sievierodonetsk after Russia destroys bridge
  • Google pays US$118 million to settle gender discrimination lawsuit that says it underpaid women
  • Teen used small drone to help Ukraine’s forces target Russian tanks and trucks
  • Amnesty accuses Russia of war crimes in Ukraine’s Kharkiv, where civilians died in ‘indiscriminate’ attacks
  • Amazon mystery: Hope fades as belongings of missing British journalist Dom Phillips and expert Bruno Pereira are found
  • John Allen resigns as Brookings president amid FBI foreign lobbying probe
  • World headed for new era of nuclear rearmament, SIPRI says
  • January 6 panellists: enough evidence to indict Donald Trump
  • Emmanuel Macron’s majority in doubt after first round of French parliament vote
  • Tajikistan security services kill two ‘criminal leaders’ in restive east
  • Five teens shot in Kentucky after anti-gun rally
  • ‘No Rwanda’ protest held at UK immigration removal centre
  • US senators reach limited deal addressing gun violence after mass shootings
  • Dozens still missing two months after South Africa’s worst floods killed hundreds
  • Ukraine war: Pro-Russian separatists uphold foreigners’ death sentences
  • WTO holds high-stakes meeting: pandemic, war in Ukraine, food shortages on agenda
  • US white supremacists arrested near Idaho gay pride event, say police
  • Chinese cryptocurrency-mining rig maker Nano Labs eyes US$50 million Nasdaq IPO defying clampdown at home
  • Goldman sees end of selling in Chinese stocks, but CCB, Everbright say only volatility is assured
  • Recession fears are growing, but the global growth outlook isn’t all bad
  • Hong Kong stocks slide by most in 5 weeks as Alibaba, tech peers sink on China lockdown concerns and Fed tightening path
  • Electric Last Mile plans to liquidate, becoming the first SPAC-tied EV start-ups to go bust
  • Microsoft’s new Xbox Game Pass slate boosts ‘Netflix of gaming’ with blockbuster titles
  • Future of work: How can businesses Achieve Positive-Sum Growth using AI-Driven Solutions
  • China’s US$1.3 trillion stock rebound rewards contrarian funds with three barriers challenging path to next level
  • China’s economy faces headwinds, but don’t fear another 2015 crisis
  • Outdoor gear retailer Beneunder sparks ire of Communist Party mouthpiece People’s Daily as it seeks Hong Kong IPO
  • Building an entrepreneurial mindset in the young to harness the potential of the Greater Bay Area
  • From high cholesterol, high blood pressure and prediabetes to healthiest he’s ever been – how an ex-CEO turned his health around
  • Chinese Nezha legend inspires Hong Kong Dance Company production that’s inventive and inspiring
  • Luxury dog kennel design competition draws Foster + Partners architects, former Apple design chief and bespoke furniture maker David Linley among others
  • A dream come true for Gin Lee: pop, jazz and tango battle it out in a Le French May concert in Hong Kong unlike anything she has done before
  • Will Cyprus’ City of Dreams Mediterranean casino resort be a safe bet, with no Russian gamblers and fears of money laundering?
  • Procter & Gamble Hong Kong and Mannings take Japanese premium hair care brand Hair Recipe to new heights
  • What Vietnam’s first luxury train carriage is like to ride – a soothing, at times surreal, journey between Da Nang and Quy Nhon
  • Is Hong Kong cinema dead in an era of mainland China co-productions? No, an exciting new generation of local filmmakers says
  • Domestic abuse in ancient China was common and a woman’s punishment for divorcing her husband was harsh, but one woman said ‘enough’
  • The 8 best steak and grill restaurants in Hong Kong, from the Grand Hyatt Steakhouse and Ocean Park Marriott Hotel’s Prohibition Grill House & Cocktail Bar, to Rosewood Hong Kong’s Henry
  • 5 scandalous K-pop idols who fans urged to leave their groups: Lesserafim’s Garam, NCT’s Lucas, Exo’s Chen and Chanyeol, and Red Velvet’s Irene all went on hiatus
  • The problem with fashion NFTs? You can’t wear them everywhere – Nike, Burberry and Dolce & Gabbana may be cashing in on digital wearables, but users need to pick their metaverse
  • Elon Musk vs Bill Gates – what are they fighting about now? The billionaire Microsoft and Tesla founders’ Twitter beef over Covid-19, bitcoin, electric cars, space travel, climate change ... and more
  • Inside Tom Cruise’s multimillion-dollar collection of cars, motorbikes and planes: the Top Gun: Maverick star owns luxury BMWs from Mission: Impossible, rare Porsches and even a vintage WWII jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • COVID-19 cases in Tokyo under 1,000 for 1st time in 5 months
  • Liver-disease problems rise as more people drink at home
  • Married couple to launch legal challenge to keep surnames
  • Nuke evacuees allowed to go home in ‘difficult-to-return’ zone
  • Grandson of officer in Dreyfus affair talks to class in Japan
  • Weather experts warn about dangerously wet early July period
  • Japan toughens defamation penalties after wrestler’s suicide
  • Small protests in Tokyo and Sapporo against Olympic bid
  • COVID-19 cases mark 30th day of week-on-week decline in Tokyo
  • Cheering fans at soccer stadium herald a return to pre-pandemic era
  • Tamaki to seek re-election in Okinawa with focus on economy
  • Elderly rickshaw puller the main draw in secluded Yamagata spa
  • Kishi voices concerns about China’s military drills near Japan
  • Kishi: Japan, China agree to boost defense dialogue
  • Japan to resume joint military drills with U.S. and South Korea
  • Yen falls to lowest level since 1998 as U.S. yields march higher
  • Japan celebrates having the most companies that are a century old
  • Virus cluster at nightclub sets off new Beijing clampdown
  • Chinese Canadian pop star Kris Wu faces rape charge in court
  • China accuses U.S. of trying to ‘hijack’ support in Asia
  • North Korea plans crackdown as Kim pushes for internal unity
  • Seoul: N. Korea fires suspected artillery pieces into sea
  • North Korean leader reaffirms arms buildup in party meeting
  • India, China growing markets for shunned Russian oil
  • Myanmar junta can’t beat rebels, should restore democracy--U.S. diplomat
  • Ukraine, U.S.-China relations dominate Asian security meeting
  • Zelenskyy tells Asian meeting: Stopping Russian invasion crucial for whole world
  • U.S. buys more monkeypox vaccine as global case count grows
  • Global nuclear arsenal to grow for first time since Cold War--think-tank
  • As fighting rages, Ukraine’s leader says troops defy Russia
  • Fossilized bones of mysterious marine mammal found in Gifu
  • DNA study offers third theory on origin of extinct Japanese wolf
  • Researchers find why scratching worsens chronic itching problem
  • ‘Gundam’ episode reworked into film captures ‘essence’ of series
  • EDITORIAL: Japan should take the lead to ensure a stable Asia-Pacific
  • VOX POPULI: Let us raise our umbrellas to a Londoner who popularized them
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Senators Reach Bipartisan Deal on Gun Safety
  • Tough Questions for West as Ukraine Cities Teeter
  • They Inhabited Separate Worlds in Taiwan. Decades Later, They Collided in a California Church.
  • Poland Shows the Risks for Women When Abortion Is Banned
  • The Hotel Is 642 Feet Tall. Its ‘Architect’ Says He Never Saw the Plans.

  • Trump Campaign Chief to Headline Jan. 6 Hearing on Election Lies
  • Logan Ury Says You’re Dating All Wrong
  • Always an Outlier, Kelsie Whitmore Just Wants to Play Baseball
  • ‘A Strange Loop’ Wins Best Musical as Tonys Celebrate Broadway’s Return
  • Food Export Bans in Asia Prompt Fears of More Protectionism
  • India’s Economy Is Growing Quickly. Why Can’t It Produce Enough Jobs?
  • Here’s What You Need to Know About America’s Super-Hot Inflation
  • Go Here, Not There: Vacation Alternatives for the Budget-Conscious
  • Goldman Sachs Is Being Investigated Over E.S.G. Funds
  • Google Agrees to Pay $118 Million to Settle Pay Discrimination Case
  • Howard Schultz: Starbucks Is Battling for the ‘Hearts and Minds’ of Workers
  • Google Sidelines Engineer Who Claims Its A.I. Is Sentient
  • You Won’t Use That Cool Feature
  • Hiking Down to Phantom Ranch, the Grand Canyon’s ‘Destination Hotel’
  • Trump Is Still a Threat
  • Civil Rights Activists Fought for America’s Democracy. They Should Be Honored as Veterans.
  • She Wrote a Dystopian Novel. What Happened Next Was Pretty Dystopian.
  • Pro-Macron Forces Expected to Prevail but Face Left-Wing Challenge
  • China Won’t Hesitate to Fight for Taiwan, Defense Minister Warns
  • A Caravan of Migrants Is Heading Toward the U.S. Border
  • A Restored Painting Recalls the Colosseum’s Christian Past
  • U.S. Accelerated Expulsions of Haitian Migrants in May
  • John R. Allen Resigns as Brookings President After Qatar Revelations
  • Palin Leads Special Election for House Seat in Alaska
  • U.S. Navy Plans ‘Safety Pause’ After Series of Aircraft Crashes
  • Two Targets of Trump’s Ire Take Different Paths in South Carolina
  • Just 5 Months Into His Term, Adams Is Busy Raising Money to Win Another
  • Andrew Giuliani’s Vaccination Status Will Bar Him From Debate Studio
  • ‘A Start’: Gun Deal Stirs Hope but Also Frustration in Places Scarred by Shootings
  • Dozens of White Supremacists Arrested in Idaho Had Planned to Riot, Authorities Say
  • DeSantis Event at Chelsea Piers Faces Backlash Over L.G.B.T.Q. Rights
  • John Merriman, Eminent Historian of France, Is Dead at 75
  • Alexander Nikitin, Who Coached a Chess Champion, Dies at 87
  • Paula Rego, Artist Known for Unsettling Images, Dies at 87
  • You’re Reading One Right Now
  • Where Can I Find a Great Travel Jacket?

  • The Cubs Flirted With Success. The Yankees Define It.
  • Where Have You Gone, Arthur Ashe? LIV Tour Golfers Need You.
  • The Art World Loves Basketballs. And Hoops and Jerseys and Backboards.
  • Hostile Crowds. $3,500 Tickets. Worth It to See Stephen Curry.
  • Five Decades, Three Generations of Runners, One All-Women’s Race
  • Zahn McClarnon Sets His Thousand-Yard Stare on the Lead
  • ‘Soft’ Review: Young Black Men, Gently Pointed Toward Liberation
  • Two Gems of June: Premieres at Carnegie Hall and Harlem School of the Arts
  • What’s on TV This Week: ‘The Old Man’ and Juneteenth Specials

  • In ‘The Baby,’ Killing With Cuteness

entry.title: Taipei Times: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
